title: aphyllon californicum subsp. grayanum
intro: Aphyllon californicum subsp. grayanum, commonly known as Gray's aphyllon or California dog's-thorn, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Aphyllon californicum subsp. grayanum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, this plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
